Product Launch Formula PLC 3
By now you know that you need to launch. You know that every successful business,
every successful brand, every successful product starts with a successful product
launch. You do not want to show up slowly. You don’t want to depend on hope
marketing. That’s where you put all this time into creating your product, creating your
business and then you hope it does well. That doesn’t work so well. You need to
engineer your success. You need to properly execute a product launch to ensure your
success. This video is all about blueprinting that launch in creating your product launch
path.
I'm Jeff Walker and since 2005 I've been training entrepreneurs how to launch their
businesses and their products. I've trained thousands and thousands of clients and
they’ve done hundreds of millions of dollars in launches. Let's blueprint your launch.
First of all, down below this video you can download the actual blueprint itself. Theirs is
an actual blueprint and go ahead and download that. Open it up so you can follow
along.
A couple of warnings. First of all is that blueprint it's pretty intense. There's a lot
there. There's a lot of detail, too many pages long. Please don’t be intimidated by
that. You don’t need to know it all. You don’t need to learn it all. You don’t have to do
it all. Frankly, what we're about here is creating that blueprint so you know which
pieces that you need from the overall blueprint.
The other thing is I'm going to go very quickly in this video. I'm not necessarily going
to cover every single piece in the blueprint. I'm going to skip over some pieces.
There's going to be some things I cover in this video that are not in the blueprint and
there's going to some things in the blueprint that I don’t get to. Sorry about that but
we've got a lot of ground to cover and frankly time is getting tight.
I've already given you 2 videos. That first video was all about the sideway sales letter
and in that video we basically gave you the framework that makes these launches work.
The second video I showed you the 3 big guns of the product launch world and also
showed you the launch stacking. Launch stacking is how I went from basically a stay-
at-home dad who hadn't made any money in years and then started with this tiny little
list of 19 email addresses and built it up to where I've now got a multimillion dollar
business.
If you haven't seen those videos yet, be sure it should be up at the top of the page
check those out. They're pretty much I think required viewing, required homework if
you wanted your business to thrive for the next 12 months or if you just starting up … if
you even want your startup to get off the ground, you got to watch those videos so
make sure you watch them. They are required homework.
Like I said, I'm going to move in quickly through this because time is tight. We've got
this video. We've got the next video. We don’t have much time left here and so I'm
going to move as quickly as possible. Don’t be intimated. Remember, it is about baby
step. It's about taking that next step and that’s what I've done all along from when I

first started out all along the path until I built this business that I've got now. It is

Really, the core of this it's about connecting with your market. It's about delivering a
lot of value. It's about finding out what they want and then giving it to him and it's that
simple. Let's go ahead and get started and create your blueprint, find your product

I'm going to start with strategy because strategy is the thing that’s going to build your
business and your income over the long term. In one sense, I'm a hard core marketer
and love those tactics, change the color of this button or change the wording of this
piece right here and you're going to get up 2 percent increase. I love that kind of stuff,
but strategy is what wins in the long run so I always start with strategy.
Here are some of the underlying core strategies that really fuel everything I do with
product launches and business launches and product launch formula. First of all, you
always want to be focused and you're list building so I consider product launches are
list building and steroid. The best way to build a list is through a product launch. I've
seen this over and over and over my businesses where I can often add thousands and
thousands of subscribers to my lists within a few days.
The thing is no matter whether you have to start building or whether you've got a huge
list, you always have to focus on building your list of both prospects and clients always
as habit. If you don’t have your first subscriber yet, you have to get your first
subscriber and then you get your first 100 subscribers. If you're just starting out that
might seem like a lot.
The thing is when you get to 100 subscribers, what do you want to do? You want to
get to 1,000 subscribers. I'll tell you what. Everyone who's got 1,000 subscribers on
their list they want to get to 10,000 and they think the Holy Grail is 10,000. When you
get to 10,000, you want 100,000. No matter who you are you always have to focus on
building a list and the best way to do it is with a product launch.
It's not just the size of your list that’s important. It's the relationship that you have with
your list. The best warmest list that you can have is a launch list. That’s the list you
built throughout your launch. One of the things I pay attention to is numbers. I watch
my open rates and I watch my click through rates when I send out an email to my list.
The best click-through rates and open rates are always during and after a launch.
That’s why we call this launch list phenomenon.

Having a launch list is like having this warm security blanket because some people and
some markets open rates vary, click-through rates vary. There are some people with
bigger list that are getting 8 percent open rate but when I'm in the middle of a launch,
I'm getting 40, 50, 60 percent open rates and that’s the big lists. To get a 60 percent
open rate to a large list almost unheard of unless you're in a launch. Then when the
launch is over, it doesn’t end. It keeps on going. Those open rates stay very high and
then they gradually tailor off until you get to your next launch and then boom you have
that super warm list again.
Creating raving fans is thus the name of the game. These are people that are going to
come back and buy from you again and they're going to tell other people about you. If
you can create raving fans, then you win and the people go through a launch with you.
The prospects, the clients, they go through a launch with you. They love you because
you're providing great value to them. You're delivering that problem solution path for
them. You're delivering great content. You're building value in their life. They love
you. That’s why you have the super warm relationship with your list. Even the people
that don’t buy from you will still love you.
Then these next 2 I'm just going to group them together, building a crushing offer and
identifying deadly effective sales hooks. This is all part of what we call the launch
conversation. When you start putting out your pre-launched videos, your pre-launched
content, you end up creating this conversation because people will leave comments.
They’ll interact with you on social media. They’ll send emails back to you and you
basically know what's ticking inside their head. You know what their problems are. You
know what their hopes and dreams are and then you can create an offer that they
really want.
If you create an offer that people really, really want, if you can identify what they want
and give them what they want, it makes everything else easy. It makes the sales super
easy. If you can identify the objections and then answer them, it couldn't be an easier
sale. You don’t have to do some slimy sales, hard sale type of thing. You're just
identifying what their problems are. You're answering their objections and then you're
offering this great solution. That’s what their problem solution path is about.
I'll tell you that launch conversation is so powerful. I'm using it right now. As I'm
making this video I'm in a launch and I've gotten hundreds of comments, tons of social
media interactions. All kinds of email is coming back to me so I know what people are
thinking that are watching my videos and so that I can teller all my follow-ups. The
stuff that is going in this presentation, this video that you're watching is because of the
comments that were left on my prior videos. I could see what people wanted to know,
what their problems were and so then I can take it and I can mold my next video based
on that conversation. It is incredibly powerful.
You also get this massive positioning. Positioning is where people feel you as this
expert or authority or guru or a leader. When you have that position, when you have
that leadership position, that expert position, when people look to you as the go-to

solution in your market, then you win hands down. It's absolutely it is how you win in

Then these next 2 are grouped together, the product launch parachute and this no
single point of failure. This is the idea of you're nit just putting out 1 sales letter, 1
sales video on hoping it does well. I think of those Super Bowl commercials where
people spent millions of dollars to make the commercial and millions of dollars to air the
commercial. Every year there's a few winners. There are some commercials that really
resonate people love and get some bid results.
There are also some real flops, some commercials that don’t do very well. They spent
millions and millions of dollars of months or even years of preparation and then it failed.
The product launch is the exact opposite of that. This is what we call the product
launch parachute. That’s where you have all these interactions and it's a process. It's a
conversation with the market. If something doesn’t resonate, you have time to regroup
and make some tweaks based on that launch conversation so your next video will hit
any points that you missed and will speak the language that your prospects want to
hear.
There is no single point of failure. It's a very risk-adverse strategy. I'd like to say this
screw up 80 percent of it and it still works and that might be a little hyperbole. It might
not be quite 80 percent. If you get that problem solution path that I talked about in

required viewing. You got to check out those videos. If you don’t have that broken

Then there's a good old launch stacking. I went into this deep in video 2. Be sure you
watch video 2. That’s where your results grow and grow and grow, where your
knowledge of market grows, your list grows, your momentum grows. The number of
past clients, your word of mouth, your social media, it all grows from launch to launch
as you build your position, build your list and build your power within your marketer
and niche. They just stack. The launch is stacked.
That’s how I went from that. First launch is 1,650 dollars and then a few years later I
was doing a launch. It's doing 100,000 dollars. A few years later, doing launch as
millions of dollars, that’s launch stacking. You always want to be building your power

Next up we've got tactics. I've got a lot of tactics built in here. It goes real deep, but

Then there are sequences. All of these sequences are in all the different types of

What I want to point out here that I haven't mentioned out to this point is the relaunch.
The thing is once you do a launch then just a vision, more people will come in into your

become a prospect. You can often do a relaunch and it's basically you're just repeating
the launch that you did so it's almost no work. You make a bunch more money and it is
absolute complete awesomesauce. I love relaunches. It's like free money.
Now we're going to talk about your launch path. We've got 4 different paths defined
here. I know you can’t read it; it's too small. We're going to go on to detail. It's the

If you have a current business, it's awesome to be you that you're watching this video
because product launch formula is basically instant leverage for you. The way you get
the most leverage the quickest out of your business. You just basically put together a
new offer or you relaunch your current offer, you create a new bundle, you create new
pricing, you create new pricing terms or you create a new product.
If you create a new product, you probably want to do a seed launch so you can test it
out and get paid to create the product. Otherwise if you're relaunching a current offer
if you create a new bundle, then you use an internal launch. There's just really no
better way to leverage up in your business. In fact, I'm willing to bet that if you put
together a good launch, it's probably going to be the biggest week you've ever had in
your business when you open the card for your launch. This is absolute no brainer. It's
basically like having a license to print money. I love the way this fits into current
businesses.
Then if you have an un-leveraged business, by this I mean you might be a coach,
practitioner or service provider or consultant, some of that works one on one with
people, then this is how you start to leverage your time and your business. This works
in all kinds of different niches. These are just some of the big meta-niches like health
and wellness, spirituality, relationships, money, real estate, business, sports and
hobbies, works really great with sports and hobbies. This is just a small sample. It
goes much deeper than that.
If you work with people one on one, then you already have clients who probably want
to buy more stuff from you. They probably don’t have enough of you. There's
probably not enough of you to go around and certainly it's not enough of your time to
go around. Your timing is or your income and your ability to help people make a bigger

hours. You need to get leveraged to make that impact, to increase the positioning in
your market.

You really need to build your tribe. Social media is okay for that. Email is still king. I

your own products.
Building your products is easier than you think. The seed launch is amazing for that. I

Once you start creating your products, it changes the math in your business. All of a

working more hours to help more people. You can afford to buy a lead. You're no
longer trading dollars for hour. You can attract joint venture partners and affiliates, and
it can really just change the whole nature of your business.
If you are in this category once you go beyond that seed launch, once you start to build

up and create an online business, you're publishing. I'm like, "Wow. All these other

Start with the seed launch to create your first product and get your first list. I firmly

It's the quickest way to get in the game.
You need to get in the game or else you're just going to still be that wantrapreneur.
You're never going to be that entrepreneur. You're never going to move into actually

Next up and I haven't talked about this before but being an artist … Lots of PLF owners
are artists. They're writers, they're painters, they're sculptors, jewelers, recording
artists and they have had some real success. I've worked for some very, very incredibly
famous authors to help put together promotions for them.
When you're doing a launch for a creative work, you're like, "How can I create value?
How can I create prelaunch? Jeff, I see what you're doing. You're doing these
prelaunch videos that are teaching your methods. If people want to go deeper, then
they can buy your product launch formula from you. I see how you're doing it but how
am I going to do it?" I've got a novel or I've got a painting or I've got a piece of
jewelry. How can I do it?
The way it usually works is your prelaunch is about the creation of the work. You're
giving people a glimpse into the creation on how you're creating it. You are
foreshadowing the work. In other words you're giving people glimpses of the work.
Maybe a novel you're giving them the first chapter or you're dropping some hints about
some of the characters.
Talking about the creation of work, the foreshadowing of the work and then if you feel
comfortable with this, you might even want to give a little glimpse into your life as an
artist. People love that. You might be sitting there thinking, "All I do is I get up and I
walk, go down to the basement, I sit in front of my computer and I write all day." It's
like the most boring thing in the world to sit there and watch. I love it but at the end of
the day it's just me sitting around in my pajamas, typing on my keyboard. How exciting
is that?
The reality is that people that are following you they just have this romantic idea of
what your life is like as an artist. They are really interested in you. If you're willing to
give a few glimpses behind the scenes of your work in life and your creative of process,

they will eat that up and you can create a very nice sequence if you use those 3 ideas I

I want to just go through the types of launches. Some of these I've already covered so
I'm going to go super quick. If you go, check out the actual PDF blueprint. You're
going to be able to see all this work. I'm not going to go through all of them right now.
I just want to touch on a few of them.
One is what I call the shadow launch. It's fun. It's probably the easiest launch. This is
where you are an affiliate for other people's products and you do a launch. This works
with new products that are being launched and it works especially well with products
that have been on the market. In this case, you do not even have to have your own
product. You're basically launching someone else's product.
The shadow launch, it boils down to creating a sequence and some type of special offer
which is usually based around some bonus for that product and you can actually do a
launch. I've seen affiliates who … people who are affiliates and would sell a product
actually increased the results by 10 or 12 times when they put together a shadow
launch. This is how you this if you don’t even want to create your own products.
Again, I highly recommend creating your own products as it changes the math behind
your business. It gives you a lot more power, but you can also use everything I've
taught you to launch other people's products as an affiliate or as a joint venture partner.
Another launch I haven't mentioned so far is the evergreen launch. This is where you
take your one-time launch and you turn it into a profitable automated system. It's
basically a perpetual launch for new leads. People come into your world. They join
your list. They learn about you. They subscribe to your email list and then you run a
launch sequence for them based on when they joined your list.
What you're doing is you're leveraging the … If you put together an internal launcher
even a JV launch you're taking that work you're doing with the prelaunch content. You
increased the leverage because you're using that work over again and this way as new
leads come into you, you do a launch as they come in. This is an awesome business
model.
Then there's the relaunch. I mentioned this earlier so I'm not going to go back into it,
but basically it's great piece. It's almost no work and it's a lot more money.
One more is the e-commerce launch. Lots of questions about this. People absolutely
use this for e-commerce and it's great to this regularly. Lots of people are doing this on
a monthly basis or even a weekly basis. One of the keys is not even the sales you
make on a launch but driving people back to your site and keep them in the habit of
regularly coming back to your site so they're buying more stuff. The best way to think
about this is think in terms of bundles. That’s usually the easiest way to do an e-
commerce launch, bundle together some of your products. Create a bundle with a
special offer and then do a launch around that.

I want to talk about the fine print. These are the big gotchas. These are the things
that hold people back. I have been teaching product launch formula. I have been
teaching entrepreneurs how to do this launches since 2005. I've had over 10,000
clients go through my training. They’ve done hundreds of millions of dollars of
launches. I've seen people who have tremendous success. I've see other people
struggle and so I basically want to go through and cover some of the things that really
stop people or hold people back.
The first thing is his idea of I don’t have a list. I don't have a list where it's like, "Jeff,
this looks fantastic but I don’t have a list so I can’t do this." The thing is I didn’t start
with a list. No one does. Everyone starts with their first subscriber. I told you how I
started with that 19 email addresses on my list and I built it from there gradually. Then
a week or 2 later I got my 20th subscriber and then a week later my 21st subscriber.
Then eventually I started building up a list-building process so as getting 1 subscriber a
day and then 3 subscribers a day and then 15 subscribers a day and then 30
subscribers a day and gradually built over time. That list that started with 19 eventually
grew to over 80,000 subscribers. No one starts with a list. No one is born with a list.
No one comes out of the womb with a list. You have to start. When I moved away
from publishing about the stock market and I started my business where I've trained
people about how to market, I started over again. I didn’t start with a list. No one
starts with a list.
The key is that you need to start building a list. No matter what business you're in, you
need to start building a list. The best way to build a list is with a launch. That is
absolute. I have had added hundreds of thousands of subscribers to my lists in my
various businesses and I would say about 80 percent of them came through launches.
It's the best way to build a list.
Just saying if you actually register for my PLF, product launch formula coaching
program, I do include a complete list building course as a bonus with product launch
formula. You also in addition to product launch formula you get a list building course
that teaches you how to build a list and how to build a relationship with that list.
The next one is I don’t have a product. I already talked about this quite a bit n this
video and the thing is, again, I didn’t start with a product. No one starts with a
product. No one does. You have to create them and it's just not that hard especially
with the seed launches, the easiest way to create a product. If you can talk on the
phone, you can create a product. You really do want to create your own products
because it's the only way you truly have control in your business. If you're selling
someone else's product, promoting someone else's product, that product could go
away. The product quality could go down the drain. They stop paying you to promote
so you need to create your own product.
You can launch almost any kind of product or service. It doesn’t matter. I have people
launched offline services, online services, online products, offline products, actual
physical widgets, pieces of art, membership sites, DVD course, online courses, training,

The key things you do want to get that position of being seen as an expert or guru or
authority or as a leader because they'll make your life easier. You can charge higher
prices. You get more traffic. You get featured at another programs. You get featured
for interviews. You get featured in books, featured in media. You can attract partners.
You can attract deals. It's way better being seen as the authority in your market.
Next up is this is a silly one. I met people look at what I'm doing and they see this. I
actually do what I teach and they see me teaching marketing. They see all other
people teaching business building and marketing and think that works great if you're in
that market, if you're teaching people about business but I'm teaching people about
how to have a deeper meditation practice.
The thing is is if you're in a different market besides teaching marketing or teaching
business, it really is awesome to be you because these techniques work better and
they're easier to use when you're outside of the marketing, when you're outside the
business building niche. Frankly, there are so many launches. There are so much noise
in the market that I'm in that’s teaching people how to build their business.
It's a lot easier if you're teaching people about growing daffodils. It's a lot easier if
you're teaching people about playing guitar, if you're teaching people about meditation.
Much, much easier if you're in those markets. Frankly, it's been using almost any kind
of market there at least just a few of them; raw food, health food, meditation, mixed
martial arts, dog training, tennis instruction, baseball coaching, soccer coaching, holistic
health, lot of people in that holistic health or that meditation that spirituality, lots of
people on that niche doing really, really well.
It's a relationship sale. Frankly, with these launches it's not a hard sale. It's a
relationship type of sale. You get massive results but it's not a hard pitch. It's not a

Next one is I don't live in the US. It's not going to work in Mexico or whatever. The
last time I opened up registration for product launch formula, we had people join from
43 different countries. This works everywhere. Everywhere you live people are going
to be more influenced like people they know, like and trust and they're going to be
influenced by those mental triggers that I already talked about a couple of times in
these videos.
No matter where you live scarcity is going to work. No matter you live social proof is
going to work, reciprocity, likability. This formula has been used everywhere; Asia,
Africa, Middle East, Europe, UK, Australia, New Zealand, Central and South America,
Canada, Mexico. Obviously, in the United States it works really, really well. It's just
been used everywhere. People are people. Certainly, there are some cultural norms
that changed but the adaptations you need are very, very small. Those mental triggers
and that people like to buy from people they have a relationship with doesn’t change.
My product is not high priced. The thing, they looked at me selling this training. My
product launch formula training is not an inexpensive training because frankly it
changes people's lives. It changes complete circumstances so it's a higher-priced
training. I compared to like what does it cost to go and get an MBA. In MBA, you
spend tens of thousands of dollars. The thing is is you do not need to have a higher-
priced product.
Bigger markets often have lower prices so I'm in a fairly small little market but if you're
in a big market like you're teaching, say, guitar where there are so many more guitar
players than there are entrepreneurs, the price has usually go down but your audience
increases. Those kind of markets often have lower prices.
Frankly, like earlier I mentioned John Gallagher, the guy that went from food stamps to
having a great business. One of his primary products is a 10-dollar-a-month
membership site. That doesn’t sound all that impressive until you start to put
thousands of people in that membership site, literally thousands. If you put 1,000
people in a 10-dollar-a-month membership site, that’s 10,000 dollars per month.
You can see how it can add up and doing a launch for a membership site or these
lower-type products like lower-priced products, it's easy to generate lots and lots of
sales so you do not need a high-priced product and your pricing will be dependent on
your market. If you're teaching meditation, it's going to be different from if you're
teaching real estate. If you're teaching guitar then if you're teaching gardening, it's just
different markets have different pricing structures.

This idea of it won’t work for me. Here this is actually a legitimate one. This is a

think you can’t, you're right." The reality is the thing that holds people back more than

mental head trash that you're going on. If you think you're just born to be a failure

If you're convinced that it won’t work for you then it won’t. The thing is is this formula

I'll never make a million dollars. A lot of people say, "Jeff, you've got a huge business.

make a million bucks." You know the fact is you're probably right. Most people don’t

You don’t have to make millions to be super successful. When I started out, I didn’t

there's personal success stacking where just people keep on building their successes.
That’s why I'm in this business is to create that kind of result for you. I love my

but I love that impact that I make on people especially the folks that are starting out. I

The final gotcha is that it's out of my comfort zone, "Jeff, I just don’t know. I'm really
